results.title: Chevening Scholarship 2026 in the UK: Your Comprehensive Guide - Scholarships Future
results.author: Md Nazmul Hossain
results.text:
Chevening Scholarship 2026 in the UK: Your Comprehensive Guide - Scholarships Future

The Chevening Scholarship 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded opportunity for international students who aspire to pursue a master’s degree in the United Kingdom. Renowned for developing future leaders and influencers globally, Chevening is highly competitive and offers not only financial support, but unparalleled access to the UK’s academic, cultural, and professional networks.

## Basic Information about Chevening Scholarships 2026

What is Chevening?

- A UK government scholarship funded by the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) and partner organizations.
- Provides fully funded one-year taught master’s degree programs at any eligible UK university for outstanding professionals with leadership potential.

2026 Application Timeline:

- Applications open: August 5, 2025, 12:00 UTC.
- Applications close: October 7, 2025, 12:00 UTC.
- Interviews: March–April 2026.
- Final Results Announced: Mid-June 2026.
- Unconditional University Offer Deadline: July 9, 2026 (17:00 BST).
- Course Start: September/October 2026.

## University/Institution Details

- Eligibility: Chevening Scholarships are valid at any UK university that offers full-time, taught master’s programs that begin in autumn.
- Course Finder: Applicants must list and apply to three eligible master’s programs via the Chevening Course Finder tool on the official website.
- Partner Institutions: Chevening partners with flagship universities like University of Kent, University of Bristol, as well as over 100 others across the UK.

## Study Areas (Available Program Names)

- Eligible Courses: Any one-year, full-time, taught master’s program at a participating UK university, with the exception of distance learning, part-time courses, and postgraduate research programs.
- Fields: Ranges widely to include Science, Engineering, Law, Social Sciences, Arts and Humanities, Business, Environment, Health, Policy, and more.
- How to Search: Use the Chevening Course Finder to locate eligible programs matching your goals.

## Scholarship Benefits

What’s Included:

- Full university tuition fees.
- Monthly living stipend, adjusted for London or outside London.
- Economy-class travel to and from your country.
- Arrival and departure allowances to help with transition costs.
- UK entry clearance (visa) fees.
- Travel top-up allowance and, where required, up to £75 for TB testing.
- Travel grant for attending exclusive Chevening events in the UK.

## What’s Not Included

- Accommodation costs: Not paid directly, but the monthly stipend is designed to help cover your rent and living costs.
- Family or dependent support: You must budget separately if bringing dependents; Chevening does not support family members.
- Personal expenses: Insurance, additional travel, or entertainment are not covered.
- Graduate Route Visa: Chevening scholars cannot apply for the post-study Graduate Route Visa and must return home for at least two years after completing the scholarship.

## Eligibility Criteria for the Chevening Scholarship

- Citizenship of a Chevening-eligible country.
- Commitment to return to your home country for a minimum of two years after the scholarship ends.
- Completed undergraduate degree (equivalent to UK 2:1 honors or university-specific requirement).
- At least two years (2,800 hours) of full-time work experience.
- Application to three different eligible UK master’s programs.
- Receipt of an unconditional offer from at least one course by July 9, 2026.
- Not previously funded by a UK government scholarship unless eligible.
- Not an employee, relative of employees, or past employee (within last 2 years) of UK government, Chevening Secretariat, or certain partner organizations (with minimal exceptions).

## Required Documents for Application

You will need to provide:

- Valid passport or ID
- Academic transcripts and degree certificates
- Curriculum Vitae (CV) or résumé
- Two reference letters (can be submitted later in the process)
- English language proficiency evidence (if required by the university)
- Conditional and unconditional offers from chosen UK master’s programs
- Professional/work experience documentation

## Scholarship Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

1. Prepare: Ensure you meet all eligibility criteria and build a strong profile with leadership experience and a clear career plan.
2. Choose Courses: Research and select three eligible UK master’s degrees.
3. Create an Account: Go to the Chevening Application website and complete personal details, including academic and work history.
4. Answer Eligibility Quiz: Complete the Chevening eligibility check.
5. Fill the Application: Write compelling essays about your leadership, networking, and career plans, and upload all required documents.
6. Submit Application: Review thoroughly for completeness and submit before the October 7 deadline.
7. Shortlisting & Interviews: If selected, attend an interview at your local UK Embassy/High Commission between March–April 2026.
8. Upload References: Submit reference letters by the required deadline if invited to interview.
9. Secure Admission: Receive and upload at least one unconditional offer by July 9, 2026.
10. Confirmation: Receive scholarship results in mid-June 2026 and proceed with visa and pre-departure arrangements.

## Tips for a Successful Application

- Start Early: Research courses and prepare documentation well ahead of deadlines.
- Personal Statement: Write a focused, authentic statement that highlights leadership, achievements, and career goals.
- Demonstrate Impact: Use specific examples to showcase how you have influenced your field or community.
- Tailor Each Answer: Address essay prompts directly and keep your narrative consistent.
- Choose Strong References: Select recommenders with direct knowledge of your accomplishments and potential.
- Proofread: Eliminate errors, and seek feedback from mentors or former Chevening scholars.
- Show Cultural Sensitivity: Demonstrate an understanding of the UK and your home country’s context.
- Plan Post-Return: Clearly outline how the scholarship will benefit your country after you return.

## Monthly Living Expenses for International Students in the UK (in USD)

| Location | Monthly Living Expenses (£) | Equivalent in USD (Aug 2025) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| London | £1,300–£1,800 | $1,650–$2,285 |
| Outside London | £900–£1,100 | $1,145–$1,400 |
| UK-wide average | £1,100–£1,400 | $1,400–$1,780 |

- Exchange Rate Used: 1 GBP ≈ 1.27 USD (Aug 2025 estimate)
- Major expenses: Rent, groceries, utilities, transportation, personal items.

## Why Choose the Chevening Scholarship? (5 Top Reasons)

1. Full financial support for tuition, living costs, and travel—removing major financial barriers.
2. Unrivaled access to UK’s world-class universities, research, and cultural life.
3. Exclusive networking opportunities with fellow scholars, alumni, and UK experts and leaders.
4. Leadership and career growth—Chevening alumni are global influencers, policymakers, and industry leaders.
5. Flexible choice of master’s programs and universities, giving you control over your academic development.
6. Powerful global alumni network—more than 60,000 Chevening graduates leading change worldwide.

## Frequently Asked Questions About the Chevening Scholarship

1. Does Chevening cover full tuition fees?Yes, it covers full tuition for a one-year master’s program.

2. Does the stipend cover all my costs, including accommodation?The monthly stipend is intended to cover major living expenses, including rent. However, budgeting is important, especially for students studying in London, where costs are higher.

3. Can I bring my family with me?No, Chevening does not cover dependent or family costs.

4. Can I apply for more than one course?Yes, you must list three eligible programs with your application and ensure at least one unconditional offer by the deadline.

5. Do I need to return to my country after my studies?Yes, you must commit to return to your home country for at least two years after completing your degree, as part of the scholarship agreement.

6. Can I work in the UK after my studies?No, Chevening scholars are not eligible for the UK’s Graduate Route Visa and are expected to return home.

7. How competitive is the scholarship?Very. Over 1,000 scholarships are awarded yearly from many thousands of applicants globally.

8. What if my country’s deadline is different?Double-check your country/territory’s application page for specific variations or updates.

Bottom Line: If you’re a motivated, ambitious leader with a clear vision for positive change in your country, the Chevening Scholarship 2026 provides a comprehensive pathway to a world-class UK education, invaluable networking, and lasting professional growth. Prepare your application early, align your goals, and take the next step toward becoming a global changemaker!

results.title: DAAD Scholarships 2026: Complete Guide to All Programs | Study Abroad
results.author: Laura Fischer
results.text:
DAAD Scholarships 2026: Complete Guide to All Programs | Study Abroad

Finance March 26, 2026

# DAAD Scholarships 2026: All Programs Explained

The DAAD offers over 100,000 scholarships yearly, from €861 to €1,200/month. Full breakdown of every program, eligibility, amounts, and deadlines for 2026.

|

March 26, 2026

|

16 min read

| Finance Updated April 26, 2026

Reviewed by Sebastian Mayer on March 26, 2026

On this page

- DAAD Scholarship Overview: Programs at a Glance
- Study Scholarships (Studienstipendien)
- Research Grants (Forschungsstipendien)
- RISE Germany and RISE Professional
- University Summer Courses
- Helmut Schmidt Programme (Public Policy and Good Governance)
- How DAAD Scholarships Compare to Other Funding
- Application Tips from DAAD Selection Committee Members
- Combining DAAD with Other Funding Sources
- Life in Germany on a DAAD Scholarship
- DAAD for Specific Nationalities
- After the DAAD Scholarship: Staying in Germany
- Frequently Asked Questions
- Next Steps

TL;DR

- DAAD awards 100,000+ scholarships per year — monthly stipends run €861 (RISE interns) to €1,407 (postdocs)
- Study Scholarship (Studienstipendium) pays €934/month plus health insurance, travel, and a €460 study allowance
- Main application deadline: October 15 for programs starting the following October
- DAAD scholarships cannot be combined with other German government scholarships, but stack with Deutschlandstipendium (€300/month)
- German language not required for English-taught programs — DAAD funds a 2-month language course before you start

The German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD source) is the world's largest funding organization for international academic exchange. It awards over 100,000 scholarships and grants each year to students, researchers, and academics from every country. With monthly stipends ranging from €861 for undergraduates to €1,200 for doctoral candidates—plus health insurance, travel allowances, and tuition waivers—DAAD scholarships rank among the most generous in Europe. If you plan to study in Germany, the DAAD is likely your single most important funding source.

This guide covers every major DAAD scholarship program available to international students in 2026. We break down the Studienstipendien (study scholarships), Forschungsstipendien (research grants), RISE internships, language course funding, and specialized programs like the Helmut Schmidt Programme. Each section lists exact amounts, eligibility criteria, application materials, and deadlines.

## DAAD Scholarship Overview: Programs at a Glance

| Program | Target Group | Monthly Stipend | Duration | Application Deadline |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Study Scholarships (Studienstipendien) | Graduates from all countries | €934/month | 10–24 months | October 15 (most programs) |
| Research Grants (Forschungsstipendien) | Doctoral candidates, postdocs | €1,200/month (doctoral), €1,407/month (postdoc) | 7–24 months (doctoral), 1–6 months (postdoc) | October 15 |
| RISE Germany | Undergraduates in STEM | €861/month | 2–3 months (summer) | December 15 |
| RISE Professional | Undergraduates/recent graduates | €861/month | 1.5–6 months | February 28 |
| University Summer Courses | Undergraduates, graduates | €1,134 total | 3–4 weeks | December 1 |
| Helmut Schmidt Programme | Professionals from developing countries | €934/month | 12–36 months | July 31 |
| Study Visits for Groups | Student groups with faculty | Varies | 7–12 days | March 1 / September 1 |

## Study Scholarships (Studienstipendien)

This is the DAAD's flagship program for international students pursuing a full master's degree in Germany. The scholarship covers the entire duration of your degree (typically 18–24 months) and includes:

- €934 per month living allowance
- Health, accident, and personal liability insurance
- Travel allowance (amount depends on home country; €250–1,075 per trip)
- One-time study allowance of €460
- Monthly rent subsidy if applicable
- Two-month German language course before the semester begins
- Tuition waiver at public universities (most charge none anyway)

### Eligibility

You must hold a bachelor's degree completed no more than six years before the start of the scholarship. The degree must qualify you for a master's program at a German university. You need a German university admission letter or proof of application. Language requirements depend on the program: German-taught programs require at least B2 German (TestDaF or DSH); English-taught programs require IELTS 6.5+ or TOEFL iBT 90+.

The DAAD accepts applicants from all countries, but selection criteria and quotas vary by region. Applicants from developing countries may have access to additional programs (see Helmut Schmidt Programme below). Some programs are restricted to specific fields of study.

### Application Materials

The standard application package includes:

- Online application form via the DAAD portal
- Hand-signed CV (tabular, DAAD format)
- Motivation letter (maximum 2 pages, explaining your study plan and career goals)
- Academic reference letters (2, from university professors)
- Certified copies of university degrees and transcripts
- Language certificates
- Letter of admission from a German university (or proof of application)
- Research proposal (for research-oriented master's programs)

### Selection Process

Applications are reviewed by independent academic selection committees organized by the DAAD. Committees include German professors and experts in the relevant field. Selection criteria include academic excellence (roughly 40% of the evaluation), the quality and feasibility of your study or research plan (30%), motivation and personal suitability (20%), and language skills (10%). An interview is required for shortlisted candidates from some countries.

### Deadlines

The main deadline is October 15 for programs starting the following academic year (October). Some programs and country-specific scholarships have different deadlines. Check the DAAD scholarship database (www.daad.de/en/study-and-research-in-germany/scholarships/) for your specific program. Results are announced in March–April.

## Research Grants (Forschungsstipendien)

These grants fund doctoral research and postdoctoral stays at German universities and research institutions. They are the DAAD's primary tool for attracting international researchers to Germany.

### Doctoral Research Grants

The doctoral grant provides €1,200 per month for 7–24 months. Additional benefits match the study scholarship package: insurance, travel, study allowance, and language course funding. You must have a confirmed doctoral supervisor at a German university or research institution (Max Planck, Fraunhofer, Helmholtz, or Leibniz). The supervisor's confirmation letter is a mandatory part of the application.

Two models exist for doctoral research in Germany:

- Individual doctorate (Individualpromotion): You work directly with a professor on your research topic. This is the traditional German model. The DAAD grant funds your stay at the German institution.
- Structured doctoral program: You enroll in a graduate school or research training group (Graduiertenkolleg) with coursework, seminars, and cohort-based supervision. These programs increasingly offer English-language tracks.

### Postdoctoral Research Grants

Postdocs receive €1,407 per month for stays of 1–6 months. The grant enables short-term research collaborations, access to German laboratories and archives, and networking with German academics. You must hold a PhD completed within the last four years and have a confirmed research host at a German institution.

### Bi-National Doctoral Programs

The DAAD also funds "sandwich" doctoral programs where you split your time between your home country and Germany. These grants cover the German phases of your doctorate (typically 6–18 months total). This model suits students who want to maintain ties to their home institution while benefiting from German research infrastructure.

## RISE Germany and RISE Professional

### RISE Germany

Research Internships in Science and Engineering (RISE) places undergraduate students from North America, the UK, and Ireland in German research laboratories for summer internships. The program matches students with doctoral supervisors at German universities and research institutions. Interns receive €861 per month, and the DAAD arranges housing assistance and cultural programming.

Eligibility: You must be enrolled as a full-time undergraduate in a STEM field at a university in the US, Canada, UK, or Ireland. Minimum GPA requirements vary but a 3.0/4.0 (or equivalent) is a reasonable benchmark. No German language skills are required—research is conducted in English. Apply by December 15 for the following summer.

### RISE Professional

RISE Professional places students and recent graduates in internships at German companies and research organizations. Unlike RISE Germany (which is academic), RISE Professional focuses on industry experience. Internships last 1.5–6 months. The same €861/month stipend applies. Apply by February 28.

## University Summer Courses

The DAAD funds participation in intensive German language and subject-specific summer courses at German universities. Scholarships cover course fees and provide a flat €1,134 for travel and living expenses. Courses run 3–4 weeks during the summer and combine language instruction with cultural programming. Eligibility requires enrollment at a university in your home country and at least A2-level German. Apply by December 1.

## Helmut Schmidt Programme (Public Policy and Good Governance)

This program funds master's degrees in public policy, economics, law, and related fields for professionals from developing and emerging countries. Named after former German Chancellor Helmut Schmidt, the program targets future leaders who will return to their home countries to drive reform and development.

Benefits match the standard study scholarship (€934/month plus insurance, travel, and language course). The program is open to professionals with at least two years of work experience in the public sector, NGOs, or related fields. Programs are taught in English at selected German universities. Apply by July 31.

## How DAAD Scholarships Compare to Other Funding

| Scholarship | Monthly Amount | Tuition Covered | Insurance | Travel | Countries Eligible |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| DAAD Studienstipendien | €934 | Yes (waiver) | Yes | Yes | All |
| Erasmus+ (to Germany) | €420–540 | Yes (exemption) | No | Partial | EU/EEA |
| Deutschlandstipendium | €300 | No | No | No | All (enrolled in Germany) |
| Friedrich Ebert Foundation | €850 | No | No | No | All (with social engagement) |
| Konrad Adenauer Foundation | €934 | No | No | Partial | All (with civic engagement) |

The DAAD stands out for its comprehensive package. The combination of monthly stipend, insurance, travel allowance, language course, and study allowance means you rarely need supplementary funding. Read our full guide on costs and funding for studying in Germany to understand how the DAAD stipend fits into a realistic monthly budget.

## Application Tips from DAAD Selection Committee Members

Based on published DAAD guidance and alumni feedback, these strategies improve your application:

- Be specific in your motivation letter. Name the exact program, professors, and research groups at your target university. Generic statements about "Germany's excellent education" signal a weak application.
- Explain why Germany, not just why a master's degree. The committee wants to know what Germany offers that your home country or other destinations do not. Reference specific labs, industry clusters, or academic traditions.
- Connect your study plan to your home country. The DAAD values applicants who plan to apply their German education in their home country or to international development. A clear return narrative strengthens your application.
- Choose referees who know your work. A detailed letter from an assistant professor who supervised your thesis outweighs a vague endorsement from a department head you never worked with.
- Start learning German. Even for English-taught programs, demonstrating German language interest (even A1) shows commitment. The DAAD funds a pre-semester language course, but arriving with some German gives you an edge.
- Apply for university admission early. Having a confirmed admission letter at the time of your DAAD application significantly strengthens your candidacy. The committee wants to fund students who will actually enroll.

## Combining DAAD with Other Funding Sources

DAAD scholarships generally cannot be combined with other German government scholarships (e.g., political foundation scholarships). You can combine them with:

- Deutschlandstipendium (€300/month, merit-based, awarded by universities)
- Part-time work (up to 20 hours/week on a student visa)
- Home country scholarships that allow international stacking
- Conference and research travel grants from the DAAD itself (separate from the main scholarship)

The Deutschlandstipendium deserves special mention. It is a merit-based scholarship awarded directly by German universities, funded 50% by private donors and 50% by the federal government. At €300/month, it is modest, but it stacks on top of the DAAD stipend. Apply through your German university after enrollment. Selection criteria emphasize academic performance, social engagement, and personal background.

## Life in Germany on a DAAD Scholarship

Is €934/month enough to live in Germany? In most cities, yes. Our cost guide shows that monthly expenses in affordable cities like Leipzig, Dresden, or Chemnitz range from €650 to €850. In Munich, Berlin, or Hamburg, you will spend €900 to €1,400. The DAAD stipend covers expenses in all but the most expensive cities, especially because health insurance is already included.

DAAD scholarship holders receive support beyond money. The DAAD Alumni Network connects current and former scholars across 200 countries. Regional DAAD offices in Germany organize networking events, career workshops, and excursions. Many scholarship holders describe the DAAD community as one of the most valuable aspects of their time in Germany.

## DAAD for Specific Nationalities

The DAAD maintains bilateral programs with many countries that offer additional or specialized funding:

- India: The DAAD-IIT Master Sandwich Programme funds Indian IIT graduates for research stays in Germany. The WISE (Working Internships in Science and Engineering) program parallels RISE for Indian students.
- Brazil: The DAAD-CAPES program funds Brazilian doctoral students for research phases in Germany.
- China: The CSC-DAAD joint program provides full doctoral funding for Chinese students at German universities.
- Egypt: The GERLS (German Egyptian Research Long-term Scholarship) funds doctoral studies in STEM fields.
- Colombia: The DAAD-COLCIENCIAS program supports Colombian doctoral candidates.

Check the DAAD office in your home country (or the DAAD website for your nationality) for country-specific programs that may offer higher stipends, additional benefits, or separate application tracks.

## After the DAAD Scholarship: Staying in Germany

Germany's 18-month post-graduation job-seeking visa applies to DAAD scholarship holders. After your degree, you can stay in Germany to search for employment related to your field of study. If you find a job, you can convert your residence permit to a work permit. The DAAD Alumni Network and career services at German universities provide job search support.

Read our guide on visas and arrival in Germany for the full process of transitioning from a student visa to a work permit.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How much does a DAAD scholarship pay per month?

Study scholarships for master's students pay €934/month. Doctoral research grants pay €1,200/month. Postdoctoral grants pay €1,407/month. RISE internships pay €861/month. All amounts are for the 2026 funding year and are subject to periodic adjustment.

### Does the DAAD cover tuition fees?

Most public universities in Germany charge no tuition. The DAAD provides a tuition waiver for programs that do charge fees (such as non-EU fees in Baden-Württemberg or TU Munich's differential fees). Private university tuition is generally not covered.

### Can I apply for a DAAD scholarship without a university admission?

Yes, but having an admission letter strengthens your application significantly. You can submit proof of application (not yet admitted) and provide the admission letter later. Some programs require admission as a precondition; check your specific program's requirements.

### How competitive are DAAD scholarships?

Acceptance rates vary by program and country. Study scholarships for master's programs typically accept 15–25% of applicants, though this varies widely by field and region. Erasmus Mundus programs funded through the DAAD have acceptance rates of 3–10%. RISE Germany is moderately competitive at roughly 30% acceptance.

### Can I work while holding a DAAD scholarship?

DAAD scholarship holders on a student visa can work up to 20 hours per week during the semester and full-time during semester breaks (up to 120 full days or 240 half days per year). Working does not reduce your scholarship amount. Many DAAD students work as research assistants (HiWi positions) at their university, which provides extra income and academic experience.

### Does the DAAD fund bachelor's degree programs?

The DAAD's primary programs focus on master's and doctoral levels. Undergraduate funding is limited to specific programs like RISE (summer internships) and university summer courses. Full bachelor's degree funding through the DAAD is rare. If you want to study an undergraduate degree in Germany, explore university-specific scholarships and the Deutschlandstipendium instead.

### Is German language proficiency required for DAAD scholarships?

For English-taught programs, no. You need IELTS 6.5+ or TOEFL iBT 90+ (or equivalent). For German-taught programs, you need at least B2 German (TestDaF 4x4 or DSH-2). The DAAD funds a two-month intensive German course before your program begins, which helps bridge the gap. Demonstrating any German knowledge, even A1, strengthens your application.

### When should I start preparing my DAAD application?

Start 12–18 months before your intended program start. For an October 2027 program start, begin preparing in spring 2026. The main deadline is October 15, 2026, but gathering reference letters, preparing your motivation letter, and securing a university admission takes months. Reference letters alone require 4–6 weeks of lead time if your referees are busy professors.

### What is the acceptance rate for DAAD Study Scholarships?

Acceptance rates vary by program, field, and country of origin. For the flagship Studienstipendien, acceptance rates typically run 15–25% globally. Some country-specific bilateral programs are more competitive at 8–15%. RISE Germany accepts roughly 30% of applicants. The Helmut Schmidt Programme is highly competitive due to its prestige and career targeting. Strong academic records (GPA in top 20% of your class), a clearly written study plan, and two substantive reference letters are the most important differentiators.

### Can I apply for a DAAD scholarship if I am already in Germany?

Most DAAD programs target applicants who are outside Germany or have recently arrived. Some programs (like Research Grants for postdocs) allow applications from within Germany. Check the specific program's eligibility conditions. If you are already enrolled at a German university, the Deutschlandstipendium (€300/month, applied for through your university) is the most accessible scholarship for in-country students.

### Does the DAAD scholarship cover the Sperrkonto (blocked account) requirement?

Yes. If your DAAD scholarship covers living expenses at or above the required level, the embassy will accept the scholarship confirmation letter instead of a blocked account (Sperrkonto). The DAAD provides a formal scholarship letter that satisfies the German embassy's financial proof requirement. You do not need to deposit €11,904 into a blocked account if you hold a DAAD scholarship that covers your stay.

### What is the Erasmus Mundus scholarship and how does it compare to DAAD?

Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degrees offer €1,400/month stipends plus tuition waiver for 1–2 year programs taught across multiple European universities. Unlike DAAD (which funds study in Germany specifically), Erasmus Mundus funds mobility across 2–4 European countries. Acceptance rates are 3–10% — more competitive than most DAAD programs. Monthly stipends are higher than DAAD's €934, but the consortium application process is more complex. Both are worth applying to simultaneously.

## Next Steps

Search the DAAD Scholarship Database (www.daad.de/en/study-and-research-in-germany/scholarships/) for programs matching your profile. Identify your target master's program at a German university and apply for admission in parallel. Contact the DAAD office in your home country for country-specific advice. Read our complete guide to studying in Germany for the full picture of what to expect.

Tags: DAAD Scholarships Germany Funding Finance

results.text:
DAAD Scholarship 2026: Requirements, Eligibility, Benefits & Deadline

Study Destination

# DAAD Scholarship 2026: Requirements, Eligibility, Benefits & Deadline

May 8, 2026

11 mins

173.5k views

Summarise with AI

Did you know? Germany welcomed 379,900 international students in the 2023/24 winter semester, marking a 3% increase from the previous year. And even bigger thing to note is that Indian students are now the largest group, with 49,483 enrollments,

A 15.1% jump from the previous year!

Many of these students are studying for free or with generous scholarships!

How’s that possible? With DAAD Scholarships. Now, here’s what makes DAAD Scholarships even more exciting:

- You can study in Germany without paying tuition fees.- You can get a fully funded scholarship that covers your living costs.- You can study at Germany’s top universities - without a financial burden.

And you don’t have to pay it back! Unlike student loans, DAAD Scholarships are grants, meaning you can focus entirely on your studies without worrying about repayment.

In this guide, we’ll walk you through everything you need to know about the DAAD Scholarship 2026 - eligibility, benefits, application process, deadlines, and tips to increase your chances of selection.

Key Highlights

DAAD (Deutscher Akademischer Austauschdienst) is the German Academic Exchange Service, offering scholarships for international students to study in Germany.

DAAD offers 100+ scholarships for bachelor's, master's, and doctoral students, covering tuition, living costs, and travel.

Most DAAD scholarships are fully funded, meaning students don't have to repay the grant.

Living expenses in Germany for international students range from INR 10-12 lakhs (€11,000-€13,000) per year, depending on location.

Top universities for the DAAD scholarship include TU Munich, Heidelberg University, Humboldt University, and RWTH Aachen.

Most DAAD scholarship applications close between October and December for the following academic year.

Acceptance rate for DAAD scholarships is competitive, with around 10-15% of applicants securing funding each year.

Table of Content

- 1. What is the DAAD Scholarship?
- 2. Why Should You Apply for a DAAD Scholarship?
- 3. Who is Eligible for the DAAD Scholarship?
- 4. DAAD Scholarship for Bachelor's in Germany
- 5. DAAD Scholarship for Master's in Germany for Indian Students
- 6. DAAD Scholarship: Eligibility & Requirements for Indian students
- 7. Documents Required for DAAD Scholarships
- 8. How to Apply for a DAAD Scholarship?
- 9. When to Apply for a DAAD Scholarship?
- 10. Factors Affecting the Selection Process of DAAD Scholarship

## What is the DAAD Scholarship?

---

DAAD (The German Academic Exchange Service) is the largest German funding organization for international students. DAAD has financed more than 2 million scholars. It provides scholarships to both international students and German students. Also, there are multiple DAAD German universities for international students to choose from.

With its headquarters in Bonn, DAAD has various regional offices and information centres in more than 60 countries. So there is a big chance that you might find a DAAD office near you. You can easily contact the DAAD local office for guidance and help related to finding a scholarship. Ensure you meet the DAAD scholarship Germany eligibility.

Yocket Fact - DAAD Germany Scholarship has grown considerably in the past few years and has a budget of half a billion euros. This money comes from the European Union and the Federal Government.

The DAAD Scholarship is a merit-based and need-based funding programme that supports students across more than 100 disciplines. The scholarship covers tuition fees (if applicable), living expenses, travel allowances, and health insurance, making it one of the most sought-after scholarships for studying in Germany.

- DAAD scholarships are available for undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral students.
- It is one of the most competitive scholarships, with thousands of applicants each year.
- Many DAAD-funded scholars secure jobs in Germany post-graduation due to strong industry ties.

You can check out the DAAD network to find out the location of a DAAD office in your country.

## Why Should You Apply for a DAAD Scholarship?

---

With full funding, the DAAD Scholarships covers tuition fees, living expenses, and travel allowances, making it easier for international students to pursue their academic goals.

In addition to financial support, DAAD scholarships open doors to top-tier German universities, allowing recipients to build global networks and gain valuable academic and professional experience.

- Fully funded scholarships: Cover tuition fees, living expenses, and travel costs, ensuring financial peace of mind.
- Study in Germany: Access world-class education and research opportunities in a country known for its high academic standards.
- Prestigious universities: DAAD scholarship recipients can study at top German universities, such as TU Munich, Heidelberg, and RWTH Aachen.
- Career prospects: A DAAD scholarship can significantly enhance career prospects by providing exposure to international academic and industry networks.

## Who is Eligible for the DAAD Scholarship?

---

There are general requirements that apply to most applicants. You must be a citizen of a country eligible for DAAD funding, have a recent bachelor's degree (for master’s scholarships), and meet language proficiency requirements in either English or German.

Many programs also require relevant work experience, particularly for postgraduate and doctoral scholarships.

- Citizenship: Must be a citizen of an eligible country.
- Academic background: Recent bachelor’s degree for master’s scholarships, not older than six years.
- Language proficiency: English (IELTS 6.0+) or German (TestDaF/DSH) depending on the program.
- Work experience: Some scholarships require at least two years of relevant professional experience.

Explore Destinations

## DAAD Scholarship for Bachelor's in Germany

---

For bachelor's programmes in Germany, DAAD offers scholarships for Indian students, such as the University Summer Courses for Foreign Students, which supports language and cultural immersion, and the Working Internships in Science and Engineering (WISE), designed for research internships in STEM fields.

Let’s check out the DAAD scholarships for bachelor courses in Germany in detail:

### University Summer Courses Offered in Germany for Foreign Students and Graduates

This programme aims to deepen knowledge of the German language and culture through summer courses at German universities.​

- Eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students with at least A2 level proficiency in German.​
- Scholarship Amount: A one-time payment of €1,192 (approximately INR 1.05 lakh), covering course fees and accommodation.​
- Funding Duration: Approximately 3 to 4 weeks during the summer.​
- Application Deadline: Typically around December 1st each year.​
- Features: Provides an immersive experience in the German language and culture, enhancing linguistic skills and intercultural competence.​

### Working Internships in Science and Engineering (WISE)

This programme supports Indian students in science and engineering to undertake research internships at German institutions.​

- Eligibility: Indian undergraduate students enrolled in programmes in engineering, mathematics, and natural sciences.
- Scholarship Amount: Monthly stipend of €861 (approximately INR 77,000), plus a travel subsidy.​
- Funding Duration: 2 to 3 months.​
- Application Deadline: Typically November 1st for internships starting the following year.​
- Features: Offers practical research experience, fostering academic collaboration and exposure to advanced research environments.​

## DAAD Scholarship for Master's in Germany for Indian Students

---

​In the academic year 2024/25, the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) awarded 316 scholarships across South Asia, with 287 of these scholarships granted to Indian students for post-graduate programmes.

For master’s programmes, DAAD offers a range of fully and partially funded scholarships, supporting students across various disciplines.

Some of the most sought-after DAAD scholarships for master's studies include:

### DAAD Helmut-Schmidt-Programme (Master’s Scholarships for Public Policy and Good Governance - PPGG)

The Helmut-Schmidt-Programme, funded by the German Federal Foreign Office, supports outstanding students from developing and emerging countries who aim to contribute to social and economic development in their home nations. Since its inception, around 800 students from over 100 countries have been funded, making it a significant opportunity for Indian students pursuing a master’s degree in Germany.

- Eligibility: Indian students with an excellent academic record in social sciences, public administration, law, or political science. And at least two years of professional experience in a relevant field.
- Scholarship Amount: INR 77,000 (€934) per month for living expenses.
- Funding Duration: 12 to 24 months, depending on the programme.
- Application Deadline: 31st July each year.
- Features: Fully funded scholarship covering living expenses, travel, and health insurance. Students will also get internship and networking opportunities in German public institutions.

### ​DAAD Development-Related Postgraduate Courses (EPOS Programme)

The EPOS Programme, funded by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development, supports professionals from developing countries in pursuing a master's degree in development-related fields such as engineering, social sciences, and environmental studies. In 2024, over 1,500 students globally benefited from this scholarship, with Indian students forming a significant share.

- Eligibility: Indian students with a bachelor's degree in a relevant field, at least two years of professional experience, and a strong academic record.
- Scholarship Amount: INR 77,000 (€934) per month for living expenses.
- Funding Duration: 12 to 24 months, depending on the programme.
- Application Deadline: October to December (varies by programme).
- Features: Fully funded scholarship covering living expenses, tuition fees, travel, health insurance, and research costs. Offers internship and research opportunities in collaboration with German institutions.

### DAAD In-Region/In-Country Programme

The DAAD In-Region/In-Country Programme provides scholarships for Indian students who wish to pursue a master’s degree at a German university or within their home region. This initiative focuses on enhancing academic capacities and fostering international research collaborations.

- Eligibility: Indian students applying for a master’s in STEM, social sciences, or agriculture, with outstanding academic performance and strong motivation to contribute to regional development.
- Scholarship Amount: INR 75,000 - 85,000 (€910 - €1,030) per month.
- Funding Duration: 12 to 24 months, depending on the course.
- Application Deadline: October to February.
- Features: Supports collaborative research between Germany and South Asian universities, with priority given to candidates focusing on sustainability, innovation, and development projects. Allows students to complete part of their degree in their home country and part in Germany.

### DAAD STIBET Scholarship (Financial Support for International Students)

The STIBET Programme provides financial assistance to academically outstanding international students who may need additional support during their master’s studies in Germany.

- Eligibility: Enrolled master’s students in any discipline at a German university. Indian students must demonstrate academic excellence and financial need.
- Scholarship Amount: INR 40,000 - 60,000 (€500 - €700) per month.
- Funding Duration: 6 to 12 months.
- Application Deadline: Varies by university; typically between March and June.
- Features: Offers a monthly stipend to help cover living expenses. Provides one-time emergency grants for students in financial distress. Available at multiple German universities.

## DAAD Scholarship: Eligibility & Requirements for Indian students

---

Since there are more than 100 scholarships provided for students from various backgrounds working in various fields, the eligibility criteria for DAAD scholarships may vary a lot. However, here are some basic eligibility criteria and requirements for DAAD Scholarships that all students should meet.

### Eligibility Criteria

The DAAD scholarships are awarded to students who want to complete a full-time course of study with mandatory attendance in Germany. The basic DAAD Scholarship eligibility criteria are mentioned below for all international students. These are applicable to procure DAAD scholarship for bachelor degree and masters degree.

- Academic Qualification - Bachelor's scholarships require 12th-grade completion, master's require a recent bachelor's degree (not older than six years), and PhD applicants must have a relevant master's degree.
- Work Experience - Many postgraduate and PhD scholarships need at least two years of professional experience in academia, research, or industry.
- Language Proficiency - English-taught programmes require IELTS (6.0+) or TOEFL (80 iBT), while German-taught programmes require TestDaF 4 or DSH-2. Some accept B1/B2 German level initially.
- Residency Requirements - For short-term scholarships (under 6 months), applicants must have lived in their home country for at least one year before applying. Long-term applicants must have completed their last degree in their home country.
- Academic Performance - Competitive scholarships require a minimum CGPA of 8.5 or 85% (or equivalent) for postgraduate studies.

### DAAD Scholarship Eligibility & Requirements

For English-taught courses - Minimum IELTS band of 6.0+ or TOEFL score of:

- 550 (paper-based)
- 213 (computer-based)
- 80 (internet-based)

For German-taught courses - One of the following:

- DSH 2 or TestDaF 4 certificate (for matriculation)
- B1 level German certificate (at the time of application, with progression to higher levels later)

## Documents Required for DAAD Scholarships

---

Submitting the right documents is crucial for a successful DAAD scholarship application. Missing or incorrect paperwork can lead to rejection, so ensure that every document is accurate and up to date. The required documents vary depending on the programme, but most scholarships require proof of academic qualifications, research proposals (if applicable), and recommendation letters.

Here are the necessary documents you need while applying for the DAAD scholarship:

- Work Experience - At least two years of relevant professional or research experience, as required by specific DAAD scholarships.
- Online Application Form - The official DAAD application must be completed and submitted online through the DAAD portal.
- CV or Resume - A detailed CV highlighting academic achievements, work experience, and extracurricular activities.
- Certificate of Enrolment - Required for currently enrolled students, confirming their current academic status.
- No Objection Certificate (NOC) - A document from the home institution permitting the student to pursue studies in Germany.
- Application Form by German Host - If applicable, this form must be completed by the German university or research supervisor.
- Invitation Letter from German Supervisor - Required for research-based scholarships, confirming acceptance from a German academic supervisor.
- Reference Letter from a University Teacher - A letter from a professor or academic mentor supporting the applicant’s qualifications.
- Letter of Motivation - A statement explaining academic goals, career aspirations, and reasons for applying for the scholarship.
- University Degree Certificates and Transcripts - Copies of previous academic certificates, including final grades and degree completion proof.

## How to Apply for a DAAD Scholarship?

---

In 2024, DAAD received over 60,000 scholarship applications globally, with acceptance rates varying based on the programme and applicant profile. Indian students, particularly in STEM and public policy fields, have seen increased acceptance, aligning with Germany's focus on global talent attraction.

Hence, applying for a DAAD scholarship is a competitive process, with thousands of students worldwide vying for limited funding. So having a clear understanding of the application process is necessary. The DAAD scholarship application can be really simple and straightforward and will hardly take 25-30 minutes.

The steps to apply for a DAAD scholarship are given below:

- Visit the DAAD Scholarship Database - Explore and shortlist scholarships that match your academic background and career goals on the DAAD scholarship portal.
- Check Eligibility & Requirements - Each scholarship has specific criteria. Ensure your qualifications, work experience, and language proficiency match the programme’s requirements
- Register and Create an Account - Sign up on the DAAD portal and complete your personal profile.
- Prepare and Upload Documents - Submit required documents such as transcripts, reference letters, a CV, and a motivation letter. Some programmes may also require a research proposal or proof of work experience.
- Submit the Online Application - Double-check all details and upload the necessary files before the deadline.
- Mail Hard Copies (If Required) - Some scholarships may require additional documentation to be sent via post.
- Await Selection & Interviews - Applications undergo multiple rounds of evaluation, with shortlisted candidates potentially being invited for interviews.

## When to Apply for a DAAD Scholarship?

---

DAAD scholarship deadlines vary by programme, but most applications open in August-October and close by November-December for the following academic year. Some programmes have deadlines extending to January-April.

- Master’s & PhD Scholarships: Typically, October to December.
- Short-Term Research Grants: Varies, usually April & October.
- Summer & Winter Courses: Deadlines fall around December to January.

Here’s a quick tip for you: Always check the specific scholarship database on the DAAD official website for exact dates, as deadlines may differ by university and course. Apply at least 6-12 months in advance for better preparation and document submission.

## Factors Affecting the Selection Process of DAAD Scholarship

---

Once you apply for the DAAD scholarships, they are evaluated by the selection committees of the particular scholarship.

While thousands of students apply each year, only a small percentage secure funding due to strict evaluation criteria. Understanding what makes an application stand out can significantly improve your chances.

The selection is generally affected by the following:

- Academic Excellence - Strong academic records, preferably with a minimum CGPA of 8.5 or 85%, improve your chances. Many programmes prioritise top-performing students.
- Research Proposal & Work Experience - For research-based scholarships, a well-structured proposal with a clear problem statement, methodology, and impact is crucial. Some DAAD programmes also require a minimum of two years of professional experience.
- Project/Research Proposal - Clearly defined goals, feasibility, and alignment with Germany’s academic and research interests can strengthen your application.
- Letters of Recommendation (LORs) - Two to three strong LORs from professors or employers that highlight your academic achievements, leadership skills, and research potential make a difference.
- Language Proficiency - Meeting the required scores in IELTS (6.5+), TOEFL (80+ iBT), DSH, or TestDaF is essential, depending on the programme’s language of instruction.
- Extracurricular Activities & Social Engagement - Leadership roles, volunteer work, or community service can make your profile more attractive, especially for public policy or development-focused scholarships.
- Alignment with DAAD Objectives - DAAD prefers candidates who aim to contribute to their home country’s development post-studies. Your motivation letter should clearly reflect this goal.

## From the Desk of Yocket

---

The DAAD Scholarship is a game-changer for students who dream of studying in Germany without financial stress. But hey, securing one is not easy. The competition is fierce, and only the most well-prepared applications make the cut. It’s not just about grades; it’s about showcasing your purpose, impact, and potential. If you are applying, you need to ask yourself: Does my application tell a compelling story? Does it show how I stand out? The DAAD selection committees look at how you can contribute to your field and your home country.

If you're serious about studying in Germany and want personalised guidance on scholarships, applications, and university shortlisting, Yocket Premium can help. With expert counselling, document reviews, and step-by-step application support, you’ll have a better shot at securing funding and making your German education dream a reality.

## Frequently Asked Questions about DAAD Scholarship for Indian Students

### Is there any DAAD Scholarship for Indian students?

Indian students wanting to study in Germany are eligible for around 25 DAAD undergraduate scholarship and graduate scholarships.

### Can I work while I am on my DAAD scholarship?

You can work part-time while you are on your scholarship. However, you will need the approval of DAAD and your supervisor or course coordinator. Make sure that the part-time job does not interfere with the purpose of the scholarship. The income that exceeds EUR 450 per month will be deducted from the monthly scholarship stipend.

### Are tuition fees covered under DAAD Scholarships?

DAAD Scholarships do not cover tuition fees. DAAD Scholarships mostly only cover your cost of living in Germany. Moreover, most public universities in Germany are free of tuition fees.

### Are DAAD Scholarships offered for undergraduate studies?

DAAD does not offer scholarships to undergraduate students, but it does offer various internships at higher institutions and research institutes in Germany.

### What are the selection criteria for DAAD Scholarship?

The selection criteria mostly depend on the individual’s academic achievements and the quality of the project. Better the performance, the higher the chance of getting accepted.

### What is the DAAD Scholarship Stipend amount?

The stipend amount may vary according to the programs that you have chosen. For masters, it is 861 EUR and EUR 1200 for PhD students.

### Is there an age limit for the DAAD Scholarship program?

No. There is no age limit, but the latest academic degree should not be older than 6 years.

### Can my family join me while I am on my scholarship?

There are benefits under certain DAAD scholarships if the funding is for programs longer than 6 months. If the program is less than 6 months, there are no family benefits.

results.text:
DAAD Scholarship Germany 2026: Fully Funded Masters & PhD – Apply Online

SHARE

# DAAD Scholarship Germany 2026: Fully Funded Masters & PhD – Apply Online

## Introduction

The DAAD Scholarship represents a pinnacle of academic aspiration for international students aiming to pursue advanced studies in Germany. For 2026, this prestigious program continues its legacy, offering fully funded opportunities for Master’s and PhD candidates from developing and emerging nations. Securing a DAAD scholarship is more than just financial aid; it is an entry into Germany’s world-class academic landscape, a chance to engage with cutting-edge research, and an opportunity to build a global network. This guide provides a detailed roadmap, ensuring you possess every piece of information required to navigate the application process successfully.

Contents

IntroductionKey Highlights & Scholarship SummaryCore Metadata TableEligibility Essentials TableMajor Programs & Funding Paths Table2026 Deadline Roadmap TableFinancial Benefits & Stipend AnalysisEligibility & Selection CriteriaAcademic RequirementsLanguage ProficiencyProfessional and Personal AttributesMandatory Documents (Expert Advice)The 2026 Application ProcessInsider Tips from WinnersOfficial Links & PortalsFrequently Asked Questions

## Key Highlights & Scholarship Summary

Understanding the core components of the DAAD Scholarship is the first step toward a successful application. This program offers diverse pathways for academic advancement.

### Core Metadata Table

| Attribute | Detail |
| --- | --- |
| Host Institution | German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) |
| Study Level | Master’s, PhD, Postdoctoral, Research, Short Courses |
| Subject Areas | All subjects (refer to specific program lists) |
| Duration | Varies by program (e.g., 2-12 months for research, full degree for PhD) |
| Target Nationalities | International students, primarily from developing and emerging countries |

### Eligibility Essentials Table

| Requirement | Detail |
| --- | --- |
| Age Limit | Not explicitly defined; varies by specific program guidelines. |
| Required Language | Varies by program and university; German or English proficiency typically required. |
| Minimum CGPA | Varies by program and university; a strong academic record is essential. |

### Major Programs & Funding Paths Table

| Program Name | Primary Audience | Key Perk |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Research Grants in Germany | Doctoral candidates, Postdoctoral researchers | Opportunity to realize a research project (2 to 12 months for doctorate, 2 to 6 months for early postdoc). |
| Doctoral Programmes in Germany | Graduates, Doctoral candidates | Funding for young scientists and academics pursuing a full doctoral degree in Germany. |
| Bi-nationally Supervised Doctoral Degrees / Cotutelle | Graduates, Doctoral candidates | Doctoral degrees supervised by a university teacher at home and an academic adviser in Germany. |
| Study Visits for Academics – Artists and Architects | Postdoctoral researchers, Graduates, Faculty | Enables continued education with a study visit and intensified artistic cooperations. |
| University Summer Courses offered in Germany for Foreign Students and Graduates | Undergraduates, Graduates | Scholarships for improving German proficiency and cultural knowledge. |

### 2026 Deadline Roadmap Table

| Program Type | Opening Date | Closing Date | Selection Date (Approx.) | Earliest Funding Start |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Research Grants & Study Visits (Round 1) | Varies | October 7 | February | May |
| Research Grants & Study Visits (Round 2) | Varies | April 3 | May | July |
| Doctoral Programmes & Cotutelle | Varies | October 21, 2025 | February 2026 | October 2026 |
| University Summer Courses | September 1 each year | October 30 each year | Varies | Varies |

## Financial Benefits & Stipend Analysis

The DAAD Scholarship is renowned for its comprehensive financial coverage, ensuring scholars can focus entirely on their studies and research without undue financial burden. The program is fully funded, providing substantial support for living expenses and academic costs.

- Monthly Stipend: Scholars receive a generous monthly stipend to cover living costs. This amounts to €934 for Master’s students and €1,200 for PhD candidates.
- Tuition Fees: All tuition fees at German public universities are fully waived for DAAD scholarship recipients.
- Health Insurance: The scholarship typically includes comprehensive health insurance coverage, a critical aspect of living abroad.
- Travel Allowance: An annual travel subsidy is often provided to assist with travel costs to and from Germany. Specific amounts vary by region and program.
- Study and Research Allowance: Additional allowances may be granted to support research-related expenses, such as conference attendance or material costs.
- Family Allowances: For some long-term programs, DAAD may offer family allowances for accompanying spouses and children, though specific conditions apply.

## Eligibility & Selection Criteria

DAAD seeks highly motivated and academically excellent candidates who demonstrate the potential to contribute significantly to their fields. The selection process is rigorous, prioritizing applicants who align with Germany’s academic and research objectives.

### Academic Requirements

- Applicants must hold a relevant Bachelor’s degree for Master’s programs or a Master’s degree for PhD programs, with a strong academic record.
- Specific programs may require a certain number of years of professional experience post-graduation.
- For research grants, a well-defined and feasible research proposal is paramount.

### Language Proficiency

- Proficiency in the language of instruction (German or English) is mandatory. Applicants must provide valid test scores (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL for English; TestDaF, Goethe-Zertifikat for German).
- The required level of proficiency varies by program and university. Some programs may offer preparatory language courses.

### Professional and Personal Attributes

- A clear motivation for studying in Germany and a well-articulated career plan are essential.
- Applicants from developing and emerging countries are a primary focus, reflecting DAAD’s commitment to international development.
- The selection committee often looks for evidence of leadership potential, community engagement, and a commitment to applying knowledge gained for the benefit of their home country.

## Mandatory Documents (Expert Advice)

A meticulously prepared application package significantly enhances your chances. Each document serves a specific purpose in presenting your profile to the selection committee.

- Curriculum Vitae (CV): Craft a detailed, chronological CV highlighting your academic achievements, research experience, publications, and relevant professional engagements. Tailor it to emphasize skills pertinent to your chosen program and Germany’s academic environment.
- Motivation Letter / Statement of Purpose (SOP): This is your narrative. Clearly articulate your academic and career goals, explain why you chose Germany and your specific program, and demonstrate how your past experiences prepare you for this opportunity. Show genuine enthusiasm and a clear vision for your future contributions.
- Letters of Recommendation (LORs): Secure strong, specific letters from academic professors or professional supervisors who know your work ethic and capabilities well. Ensure they highlight your intellectual abilities, research potential, and personal qualities that make you an ideal candidate for a DAAD scholarship.
- Academic Transcripts and Degree Certificates: Provide certified copies of all your academic transcripts and degree certificates. Ensure they are officially translated into German or English if the originals are in another language.
- Language Certificates: Submit valid IELTS, TOEFL, TestDaF, or Goethe-Zertifikat scores as required by your chosen program. Meeting or exceeding the minimum language requirements is non-negotiable.
- Research Proposal (for PhD/Research Programs): Develop a clear, concise, and impactful research proposal. It must outline your research question, methodology, expected outcomes, and its relevance to your field and potentially to your home country’s development.
- Passport Copy: A clear, valid copy of your passport is required for identification and visa processing purposes.

## The 2026 Application Process

Navigating the DAAD application requires careful planning and adherence to deadlines. Follow these steps to ensure a smooth application journey.

1. Identify Your Program: Research the DAAD scholarship database to find programs that align with your academic background and career aspirations. Pay close attention to eligibility criteria and specific program requirements.
2. Select a German University: For many DAAD scholarships, you must first secure admission or an acceptance letter from a German university. Begin this process well in advance of the DAAD scholarship deadline.
3. Gather Required Documents: Compile all necessary documents, ensuring they are complete, certified, and translated as specified. This includes academic records, language certificates, CV, motivation letter, and letters of recommendation.
4. Complete the Online Application: Access the DAAD portal and meticulously fill out the online application form. Double-check all entries for accuracy and completeness.
5. Submit Your Application: Upload all required documents and submit your application before the specified deadline. Late submissions are not considered.
6. Await Selection Results: The selection process can take several months. DAAD typically announces results according to the timeline provided for each program.
7. Prepare for Visa and Travel: Upon successful selection, begin your visa application process and make necessary travel arrangements to Germany.

## Insider Tips from Winners

Those who have successfully secured a DAAD scholarship often share common strategies that proved instrumental in their journey. Heeding their advice can significantly bolster your application.

- Start Early: The application process is comprehensive and demands significant time for research, document preparation, and university applications. Beginning at least 6-12 months in advance is advisable.
- Tailor Your Application: Avoid generic statements. Customize your motivation letter and research proposal to the specific DAAD program and the German university you are targeting. Show how your goals align with their offerings.
- Network with Professors: For PhD and research grants, establishing contact with a potential supervisor at a German university before applying can be a distinct advantage. A letter of invitation or supervision agreement strengthens your application.
- Highlight Relevance to Home Country: DAAD places a strong emphasis on development-related impact. Clearly articulate how your studies in Germany will benefit your home country upon your return.
- Proofread Meticulously: Errors in grammar or spelling can detract from an otherwise strong application. Have multiple people review your documents for clarity and correctness.
- Demonstrate German Interest: Even if your program is in English, showing an interest in German culture or a willingness to learn the language can be viewed favorably.

## Official Links & Portals

Accessing the correct resources is crucial for a successful application.

| Resource | Link |
| --- | --- |
| Official DAAD Website | https://www.daad.de/en/ |
| DAAD Scholarship Database (Apply Online) | https://www.daad.de/en/study-and-research-in-germany/scholarships/ |
| Want to know about more Scholarship – Click here | https://itsstudent.com/category/scholarships/ |

## Frequently Asked Questions

Here are concise answers to common inquiries about the DAAD Scholarship.

1. Is the DAAD Scholarship fully funded? Yes, it covers monthly stipends, tuition fees, and typically includes health insurance and travel allowances.
2. Are there age restrictions for DAAD scholarships? Age limits are not universally applied; they depend on the specific program and its target audience.
3. Do I need to know German to apply? Many programs are taught in English, but German proficiency enhances your experience and may be required for some courses.
4. Can I apply for multiple DAAD scholarships? You can apply for several, but you will only be awarded one DAAD scholarship if successful.
5. What is the success rate for DAAD scholarships? The competition is high, emphasizing the need for a strong, well-prepared application to stand out.
6. Is a university admission letter required before applying to DAAD? For many programs, a letter of admission or acceptance from a German university is a prerequisite for the DAAD application.
7. When do DAAD scholarship results come out? Selection dates vary by program, typically a few months after the application deadline.
8. Can I bring my family with me? Family allowances may be available for some long-term programs, but specific conditions and additional requirements apply.
9. Are there DAAD scholarships for Bachelor’s degrees? Primarily, DAAD focuses on graduate studies, but summer courses are available for undergraduates to improve German proficiency.
10. What is the DAAD EPOS program? The EPOS program (Development-Related Postgraduate Courses) is a specific DAAD offering for students from developing countries, focusing on subjects relevant to development.

results.title: Erasmus Mundus Scholarships 2026 | ScholarWing
results.author: scholarwingofficial@gmail.com
results.text:
Erasmus Mundus Scholarships 2026 | ScholarWing

The Erasmus Mundus scholarship is the most generous fully funded scholarship program the European Union offers to international students. It covers tuition, travel, visa costs, and monthly living expenses — and it does this across multiple European countries, not just one.

Most students who apply for Erasmus Mundus do not get selected. Not because they are unqualified, but because they apply to the wrong programs, write generic motivation letters, or miss deadlines they did not know existed.

This guide gives you everything — what Erasmus Mundus actually is, what it funds, which programs are open in 2026, what the selection criteria are, and exactly how to apply.

---

## What Is the Erasmus Mundus Scholarship?

Erasmus Mundus is a scholarship program funded by the European Union under the Erasmus+ framework. It funds international students to study Joint Master’s Degree programs — degrees that are taught across two or more European universities in two or more countries.

The key difference between Erasmus Mundus and other scholarships is this: you do not study at one university in one country. You study at two, three, or sometimes four universities across Europe, spending one or two semesters at each institution. You graduate with a joint degree recognized by all participating universities.

Every Erasmus Mundus program is different — different fields, different university combinations, different application deadlines. The scholarship is administered by individual program consortiums, not by a central EU body.

---

## What Does Erasmus Mundus Fund?

The Erasmus Mundus scholarship covers:

Tuition fees — full tuition at all partner universities, regardless of the fee level. Some European universities charge up to €18,000 per year for international students. Erasmus Mundus covers this entirely.

Monthly stipend — €1,400 per month for the full duration of the program. For a two-year Master’s, this amounts to €33,600 in living expenses.

Travel and installation allowance — a one-time payment of €1,000 to cover your initial travel and settling-in costs.

Visa and residence permit costs — covered by the scholarship.

The total value of an Erasmus Mundus scholarship for a two-year program typically exceeds €50,000.

---

## Who Can Apply?

Erasmus Mundus scholarships are open to students from all countries worldwide, including Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, Nigeria, Kenya, Egypt, and all other non-EU countries.

The basic eligibility requirements are:

You must hold a Bachelor’s degree — completed before the program start date. Final-year students may apply but must have their degree before enrollment.

You must meet the language requirements of the specific program. Most programs require IELTS 6.5 or TOEFL 90. Some programs accept a Medium of Instruction Certificate from English-medium universities — check the specific program requirements.

You must not have lived or worked in the program’s coordinating country for more than 12 months in the past three years. This is the Erasmus mobility requirement — it exists to ensure the scholarship reaches students who genuinely need international mobility.

Each program sets its own additional requirements — GPA thresholds, specific degree fields, work experience, or language requirements. Always check the specific program page.

---

## Erasmus Mundus Programs Open in 2026

Erasmus Mundus funds over 200 Joint Master’s Degree programs across every academic field. The following are among the most applied-to programs open for the 2026–27 intake:

Sciences and Engineering: IMRD — International Master in Rural Development EMJMD in Food Safety — across Ghent, Warsaw, and partner universities Master in Marine Biological Resources (IMBRSea) IMLEX — Intelligent Field Robotic Systems NOMADS — Master in Sustainable Architecture

Social Sciences and Humanities: EMJMD GLORE — Global Studies EUROCULTURE — Society, Politics and Culture in a Global Context EMJMD in Journalism, Media and Globalisation GEMMA — Women’s and Gender Studies IMACS — Advanced Communication Systems

Health and Life Sciences: EMJMD in Public Health in Disasters IMIM — International Master in Innovative Medicine LIVE — Languages and International Visual Cultures

Business and Economics: MESPOM — Environmental Sciences, Policy and Management International Master in Business Administration (IMBA) — multiple consortiums EMJMD in Tourism, Society and Environment

New programs are added each year. The full updated list is available at the European Commission’s EMJMD catalog at eacea.ec.europa.eu.

---

## Erasmus Mundus 2026 Deadlines

Erasmus Mundus deadlines vary by program. There is no single application deadline for all programs.

Most programs open applications between October and January and close between January and March for the following academic year’s intake. A smaller number of programs have later deadlines — April or May.

Key dates for 2026–27 intake:

Application windows typically open: October–November 2025 Early deadlines (scholarship applicants): January–February 2026 Standard deadlines: February–March 2026 Late deadlines: April–May 2026

Check the specific program’s website for exact dates. Missing a deadline by even one day means waiting an entire year.

---

## Erasmus Mundus Selection Criteria

Every Erasmus Mundus program consortium makes its own selection decisions. However, all programs evaluate applicants on broadly similar criteria:

Academic excellence — your undergraduate GPA and degree relevance to the program. Most competitive programs expect a GPA equivalent to 3.5/4.0 or above.

Motivation letter quality — this is the most heavily weighted document in most Erasmus Mundus applications. Committees read motivation letters carefully and they can identify generic, copy-pasted letters immediately.

Relevance of background — your degree, research experience, and professional background must connect clearly to the program’s focus.

Letters of recommendation — two strong letters from professors or supervisors who know your work in detail.

Language proficiency — IELTS, TOEFL, or MOI Certificate depending on the program.

Research or professional experience — not always required, but significantly strengthens applications for competitive programs.

---

## How to Apply for Erasmus Mundus 2026 — Step by Step

Step 1 — Choose the right programs

Do not apply to ten programs randomly. Research 3 to 5 programs in your field, read each program’s curriculum, check the partner universities, and confirm the program genuinely aligns with your academic background and career goals.

Apply to programs where your degree is a natural fit — not programs where your background requires significant explanation.

Step 2 — Check eligibility for each program

Confirm your degree meets the field requirement, your GPA meets the threshold, you have the required language proficiency document, and you satisfy the mobility requirement.

Step 3 — Prepare your documents

Every Erasmus Mundus application requires:

- Contact
- Bachelor’s degree certificate and transcripts (translated into English if in another language)
- CV in Europass format or standard academic CV format
- Motivation letter — specific to each program
- Two letters of recommendation
- Language proficiency document (IELTS, TOEFL, or MOI Certificate)
- Passport copy
- Some programs require a research proposal or writing sample

Step 4 — Write a program-specific motivation letter

Your motivation letter must name the specific program, explain why you chose this specific combination of universities, connect your academic background to the program’s curriculum, and explain what you will do with the degree.

A motivation letter that does not mention the specific program by name, does not reference specific courses or partner universities, and does not connect your background to the program’s focus will not be competitive.

📝 Use our Free Motivation Letter Generator to build a structured, program-specific Erasmus Mundus motivation letter.

Step 5 — Submit through the program portal

Each Erasmus Mundus program has its own application portal — usually on the coordinating university’s website. There is no central Erasmus Mundus application portal.

Submit your application before the scholarship deadline — not the regular student deadline. The scholarship deadline is earlier and is the one that matters.

---

## Tips to Increase Your Chances

Apply to multiple programs. Most successful Erasmus Mundus scholars applied to three or more programs. Apply to your top choice and two realistic backups.

Contact the program coordinator. Before applying, email the program coordinator with a specific question about the curriculum or research focus. This demonstrates genuine interest and gives you a direct contact.

Get your reference letters early. Ask your referees at least four to six weeks before the deadline. Late or rushed reference letters hurt applications.

Tailor every motivation letter. The motivation letter for each program must be completely rewritten — not the same letter with the program name changed.

Address the mobility requirement clearly. If you have lived in a EU country in the past three years, confirm you still meet the mobility requirement before applying.

---

## Use Our Free Tools for Your Erasmus Application

📝 Free Motivation Letter Generator— Erasmus-specific motivation letter 📋 Free MOI Certificate Generator— If your previous degree was English-medium 📄 Free SOP Generator— For programs that request an SOP 📑 Free CV Builder— Academic CV in Europass-compatible format 📜 Free Reference Letter Generator— For your recommenders

---

## FAQ — Erasmus Mundus Scholarships 2026

Q: Can Pakistani and Indian students apply for Erasmus Mundus?

Yes. Erasmus Mundus is open to students from all countries worldwide. Students from Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, and all other non-EU countries are eligible and actively encouraged to apply.

Q: Is Erasmus Mundus fully funded?

Yes. Erasmus Mundus covers full tuition, €1,400 per month living stipend, travel allowance, and visa costs for the full duration of the program.

Q: How competitive is Erasmus Mundus?

Very competitive. Most programs receive 500 to 2,000 applications and fund 15 to 30 scholarship students per intake. Acceptance rates for the scholarship track typically range from 2% to 6%.

Q: Can I apply for Erasmus Mundus without IELTS?

Some programs accept a Medium of Instruction Certificate from English-medium universities. Check the specific program requirements. Use our Free MOI Certificate Generator if your university qualifies.

Q: How many Erasmus Mundus programs can I apply to?

There is no official limit. Most advisors recommend applying to 3 to 5 programs in your field to maximize your chances.

Q: When do Erasmus Mundus results come out?

Most programs announce results between April and June for the following September intake.

Q: Do I need work experience for Erasmus Mundus?

Not always required, but relevant research or professional experience significantly strengthens applications, particularly for competitive programs.

---
